title: Reconciling and Versioning Parallel Naming Systems
content:
Reconciling and Versioning Parallel Naming Systems
One of the hardest tasks in the MythOS reset is reconciling over 10 parallel naming systems and ID schemas accumulated over years. Each legacy system (perhaps different projects, platforms, or naming conventions) has to be resolved into the new unified scheme without losing important information. Here we outline techniques for thread resolution, naming collapse, and taxonomy merging: • Unified Namespace and Data Model: A straightforward strategy is to design a standardized data model or namespace that will serve as the common reference frame, then map each old system into that model. This concept is akin to the Unified Namespace in data management, which harmonizes data from different systems into a well-defined structure . In practice, this could mean defining a master folder structure or database schema in MythOS that covers all entity types you care about (documents, code, media, contacts, etc.), and then writing connectors or scripts to ingest each source into this schema. For example, if you have separate contact lists, you’d create one canonical “Contact” table and import all, merging duplicates. For files, you might establish a path scheme like Project/Year/Identifier a
